what: Log in and attach the OMOP resource
title: Connecting to the federation
text: login parameters (site URLs, resource path, demo credentials) written sites.env repository steps 2–4, read rather hard-coding anything. client now connected three sites:
code: library(DSI); library(DSOpal); library(dsBaseClient); library(dsOMOPClient)  find_up <- function(f, d = getwd()) {   repeat {     p <- file.path(d, f); if (file.exists(p)) return(p)     up <- dirname(d); if (identical(up, d)) stop(f, " not found"); d <- up   } } read_env <- function(path) {   ln <- trimws(readLines(path, warn = FALSE))   ln <- ln[nzchar(ln) & !startsWith(ln, "#")]   e <- list()   for (kv in regmatches(ln, regexec("^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)=(.*)$", ln)))     if (length(kv) == 3L) e[[kv[2]]] <- kv[3]   e } cfg <- read_env(Sys.getenv("SITES_ENV", unset = find_up("sites.env")))  builder <- DSI::newDSLoginBuilder() for (S in sub("_OPAL_URL$", "", grep("_OPAL_URL$", names(cfg), value = TRUE)))   builder$append(server = tolower(S), url = cfg[[paste0(S, "_OPAL_URL")]],                  user = cfg$OPAL_USER, password = cfg$OPAL_PASSWORD,                  resource = cfg$OPAL_RESOURCE_PATH,                  driver = "OpalDriver", profile = cfg$OPAL_PROFILE) conns <- DSI::datashield.login(logins = builder$build())  # Attach the OMOP CDM resource on every server and initialise dsOMOP. ds.omop.connect(resource = cfg$OPAL_RESOURCE_PATH, symbol = "omop", conns = conns) names(conns) #> [1] "aphrc"   "dgh"     "iressef"

what: What tables does each site expose?
title: Connecting to the federation
text: dsOMOP discovers tables runtime introspecting CDM schema — hard-code OMOP version. tables visible first site: columns person table:
code: tabs <- ds.omop.tables(symbol = "omop", conns = conns) head(tabs[[1]], 12) #>              table_name schema_category has_person_id concept_prefix #> 1                person             CDM          TRUE         gender #> 2    observation_period             CDM          TRUE    period_type #> 3      visit_occurrence             CDM          TRUE          visit #> 4          visit_detail             CDM          TRUE   visit_detail #> 5  condition_occurrence             CDM          TRUE      condition #> 6         drug_exposure             CDM          TRUE           drug #> 7  procedure_occurrence             CDM          TRUE      procedure #> 8       device_exposure             CDM          TRUE         device #> 9           measurement             CDM          TRUE    measurement #> 10          observation             CDM          TRUE    observation #> 11                death             CDM          TRUE          death #> 12                 note             CDM          TRUE           note ds.omop.columns("person", symbol = "omop", conns = conns)[[1]] #>                    column_name cdm_datatype                 db_datatype #> 1                    person_id      integer                     integer #> 2            gender_concept_id      integer                     integer #> 3                year_of_birth      integer                     integer #> 4               month_of_birth      integer                     integer #> 5                 day_of_birth      integer                     integer #> 6               birth_datetime     datetime timestamp without time zone #> 7              race_concept_id      integer                     integer #> 8         ethnicity_concept_id      integer                     integer #> 9                  location_id      integer                     integer #> 10                 provider_id      integer                     integer #> 11                care_site_id      integer                     integer #> 12         person_source_value  varchar(50)           character varying #> 13         gender_source_value  varchar(50)           character varying #> 14    gender_source_concept_id      integer                     integer #> 15           race_source_value  varchar(50)           character varying #> 16      race_source_concept_id      integer                     integer #> 17      ethnicity_source_value  varchar(50)           character varying #> 18 ethnicity_source_concept_id      integer                     integer #>         concept_role fk_domain is_date is_sensitive is_blocked #> 1        non_concept             FALSE        FALSE      FALSE #> 2     domain_concept    Gender   FALSE        FALSE      FALSE #> 3        non_concept             FALSE        FALSE FALSE #> 7  attribute_concept      Race   FALSE        FALSE      FALSE #> 8  attribute_concept Ethnicity   FALSE        FALSE      FALSE #> 9        non_concept             FALSE        FALSE      FALSE #> 10       non_concept             FALSE        FALSE      FALSE #> 11       non_concept             FALSE        FALSE      FALSE #> 12       non_concept             FALSE         TRUE       TRUE #> 13       non_concept             FALSE         TRUE       TRUE #> 14    source_concept    Gender   FALSE         TRUE       TRUE #> 15       non_concept             FALSE         TRUE title: Connecting to the federation
text: ds.omop.table.stats() returns disclosure-checked row counts distinct-person counts site. GiBleed cohort sharded person disjoint slices, pooled distinct-person count sum across sites — person table reconstructs whole cohort (2 694), without site exposing shard. Distinct persons per table — person row full cohort, event tables show many persons records kind: Total rows per table:
code: sites <- names(conns) tbls  <- c("person", "condition_occurrence", "drug_exposure", "measurement")  per_site <- lapply(tbls, function(tb)   ds.omop.table.stats(tb, symbol = "omop", conns = conns)$per_site) names(per_site) <- tbls  tabulate_field <- function(field) {   m <- t(vapply(per_site, function(ps)     vapply(sites, function(s) as.numeric(ps[[s]][[field]]), numeric(1)),     numeric(length(sites))))   colnames(m) <- sites   cbind(m, pooled = rowSums(m)) } tabulate_field("persons") #>                      aphrc dgh iressef pooled #> person                 889 878     927   2694 #> condition_occurrence   889 878     927   2694 #> drug_exposure          889 878     927   2694 #> measurement            885 875     926   2686 tabulate_field("rows") #>                      aphrc   dgh iressef pooled #> person                 889   878     927   2694 #> condition_occurrence 21470 20983   22879  65332 #> drug_exposure        22348 21946   23413  67707 #> measurement          14214 13852   15987  44053

what: Most prevalent conditions, pooled across sites
title: Connecting to the federation
text: ds.omop.concept.prevalence() ranks concepts number distinct persons , combining three shards single pooled ranking. concept receives pooled count every shard reports , show conditions whose pooled count complete across sites.
code: prev <- ds.omop.concept.prevalence(   "condition_occurrence", metric = "persons", top_n = 10,   scope = "pooled", symbol = "omop", conns = conns)  subset(prev$pooled, !suppressed, select = c("concept_name", "n_persons")) #>                concept_name n_persons #> 1            Osteoarthritis      2694 #> 2           Viral sinusitis      2686 #> 3   Acute viral pharyngitis      2606 #> 4          Acute bronchitis      2543 #> 5              Otitis media      2025 #> 6 Streptococcal sore throat      1677 #> 7           Sprain of ankle      1357

title: Connecting to the federation
text: Everything came back aggregates. next page extracts person-level table — still entirely server-side — confirms landed ordinary R data frame.

what: Log in and attach the OMOP resource
title: Extracting a tailored analysis dataset
code: library(DSI); library(DSOpal); library(dsBaseClient); library(dsOMOPClient)  find_up <- function(f, d = getwd()) {   repeat {     p <- file.path(d, f); if (file.exists(p)) return(p)     up <- dirname(d); if (identical(up, d)) stop(f, " not found"); d <- up   } } read_env <- function(path) {   ln <- trimws(readLines(path, warn = FALSE))   ln <- ln[nzchar(ln) & !startsWith(ln, "#")]   e <- list()   for (kv in regmatches(ln, regexec("^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)=(.*)$", ln)))     if (length(kv) == 3L) e[[kv[2]]] <- kv[3]   e } cfg <- read_env(Sys.getenv("SITES_ENV", unset = find_up("sites.env")))  builder <- DSI::newDSLoginBuilder() for (S in sub("_OPAL_URL$", "", grep("_OPAL_URL$", names(cfg), value = TRUE)))   builder$append(server = tolower(S), url = cfg[[paste0(S, "_OPAL_URL")]],                  user = cfg$OPAL_USER, password = cfg$OPAL_PASSWORD,                  resource = cfg$OPAL_RESOURCE_PATH,                  driver = "OpalDriver", profile = cfg$OPAL_PROFILE) conns <- DSI::datashield.login(logins = builder$build()) ds.omop.connect(resource = cfg$OPAL_RESOURCE_PATH, symbol = "omop", conns = conns)

what: Build and execute an extraction plan
title: Extracting a tailored analysis dataset
text: ask two person columns rename extract: gender_concept_id becomes sex year_of_birth becomes birth_year. plan executed server, producing server-side data frame bound symbol D.
code: plan <- ds.omop.plan() plan <- ds.omop.plan.person_level(   plan,   tables = list(person = c(sex = "gender_concept_id", birth_year = "year_of_birth")),   name = "demographics")  ds.omop.plan.execute(plan, out = c(demographics = "D"), symbol = "omop", conns = conns)

what: Confirm the extract landed as a server-side data frame
title: Extracting a tailored analysis dataset
text: columns exactly two asked , new names — identifier column came along: dimensions, pooled across three sites, recover full cohort 2 694 persons: ds.summary() reports D ordinary data.frame — point OMOP layer hands standard DataSHIELD toolchain:
code: ds.colnames("D") #> $aphrc #> [1] "sex"        "birth_year" #>  #> $dgh #> [1] "sex"        "birth_year" #>  #> $iressef #> [1] "sex"        "birth_year" ds.dim("D", type = "combine") #> $`dimensions of D in combined studies` #> [1] 2694    2 ds.summary("D")[[1]] #> $class #> [1] "data.frame" #>  #> $`number of rows` #> [1] 889 #>  #> $`number of columns` #> [1] 2 #>  #> $`variables held` #> [1] "sex"        "birth_year"

title: Extracting a tailored analysis dataset
text: use plain dsBaseClient. pooled mean quantiles birth_year combine sufficient statistics three sites:
code: ds.mean("D$birth_year", type = "combine")$Global.Mean #>                 EstimatedMean Nmissing Nvalid Ntotal #> studiesCombined      1958.066        0   2694   2694 ds.quantileMean("D$birth_year", type = "combine") #>       5%      10%      25%      50%      75%      90%      95%     Mean  #> 1922.941 1936.982 1949.642 1960.656 1970.000 1976.000 1979.000 1958.066

title: Extracting a tailored analysis dataset
text: extract now ordinary federated data frame. next page fits regression model ds.glm.

what: Log in, attach, and re-extract the demographics table
title: Federated regression on an extracted table
code: library(DSI); library(DSOpal); library(dsBaseClient); library(dsOMOPClient)  find_up <- function(f, d = getwd()) {   repeat {     p <- file.path(d, f); if (file.exists(p)) return(p)     up <- dirname(d); if (identical(up, d)) stop(f, " not found"); d <- up   } } read_env <- function(path) {   ln <- trimws(readLines(path, warn = FALSE))   ln <- ln[nzchar(ln) & !startsWith(ln, "#")]   e <- list()   for (kv in regmatches(ln, regexec("^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)=(.*)$", ln)))     if (length(kv) == 3L) e[[kv[2]]] <- kv[3]   e } cfg <- read_env(Sys.getenv("SITES_ENV", unset = find_up("sites.env")))  builder <- DSI::newDSLoginBuilder() for (S in sub("_OPAL_URL$", "", grep("_OPAL_URL$", names(cfg), value = TRUE)))   builder$append(server = tolower(S), url = cfg[[paste0(S, "_OPAL_URL")]],                  user = cfg$OPAL_USER, password = cfg$OPAL_PASSWORD,                  resource = cfg$OPAL_RESOURCE_PATH,                  driver = "OpalDriver", profile = cfg$OPAL_PROFILE) conns <- DSI::datashield.login(logins = builder$build()) ds.omop.connect(resource = cfg$OPAL_RESOURCE_PATH, symbol = "omop", conns = conns)  plan <- ds.omop.plan() plan <- ds.omop.plan.person_level(   plan,   tables = list(person = c(sex = "gender_concept_id", birth_year = "year_of_birth")),   name = "demographics") ds.omop.plan.execute(plan, out = c(demographics = "D"), symbol = "omop", conns = conns)

title: Federated regression on an extracted table
text: OMOP encodes sex concept id (8507 male, 8532 female). recode 0/1 male indicator bind onto extract form model frame DF:
code: ds.recodeValues(var.name = "D$sex",                 values2replace.vector = c(8507, 8532),                 new.values.vector    = c(1, 0),                 newobj = "male", datasources = conns) ds.dataFrame(x = c("D", "male"), newobj = "DF", datasources = conns) ds.colnames("DF")[[1]] #> [1] "sex"        "birth_year" "male"

what: Linear model: birth year by sex
title: Federated regression on an extracted table
text: Gaussian GLM birth_year ~ male, pooled across three sites. single 0/1 predictor intercept mean birth year women slope men-minus-women difference, fit directly interpretable:
code: g <- ds.glm(formula = "DF$birth_year ~ DF$male", family = "gaussian",             datasources = conns) g$coefficients #>                 Estimate Std. Error      z-value   p-value    low0.95CI #> (Intercept) 1957.8514202  0.4362186 4488.2344511 0.0000000 1956.9964475 #> DF$male        0.4385116  0.6229477    0.7039301 0.4814763   -0.7824434 #>              high0.95CI #> (Intercept) 1958.706393 #> DF$male        1.659467

what: Logistic model: sex by birth year
title: Federated regression on an extracted table
text: model frame supports binomial GLM just readily:
code: gb <- ds.glm(formula = "DF$male ~ DF$birth_year", family = "binomial",              datasources = conns) gb$coefficients #>                  Estimate  Std. Error    z-value   p-value low0.95CI.LP #> (Intercept)   -3.32848244 4.672977755 -0.7122830 0.4762896 -12.48735054 #> DF$birth_year  0.00168016 0.002386432  0.7040469 0.4814036  -0.00299716 #>               high0.95CI.LP      P_OR low0.95CI.P_OR high0.95CI.P_OR #> (Intercept)      5.83038566 0.0346069   3.774078e-06       0.9970717 #> DF$birth_year    0.00635748 1.0016816   9.970073e-01       1.0063777

title: Federated regression on an extracted table
text: models standard dsBaseClient fits computed dsOMOP extract: OMOP layer produced analysis dataset, ordinary federated toolchain took .

code: @Manual{,   title = {FederatedAnalysisDataSHIELD: Federated Analysis using DataSHIELD — Reproducibility Package},   author = {David {Sarrat González} and Michael Ochola},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://github.com/APHRC-DSE/Federated-Analysis-using-DataSHIELD-I}, }

title: Federated Analysis using DataSHIELD
text: Reproducibility package methods paper “Federated Analysis using DataSHIELD”. stands fully local, three-site federation runs real federated analysis OMOP CDM data using DataSHIELD dsOMOP v2.0.0. synthetic OHDSI GiBleed cohort (2 694 persons) sharded person across three independent sites — aphrc, dgh, iressef — every person records live exactly one site. client never sees patient-level data: dsOMOP returns disclosure-checked aggregates, one person-level table extracts analysed place server standard dsBaseClient. pooled person count across three shards reconstructs whole cohort (2 694) without site holding shard.

title: Federated Analysis using DataSHIELD
text: site independent easy-opal instance: Opal + MongoDB, plus Rock R-server profile named omop built dsOMOP image. self-managed PostgreSQL holds site’s OMOP CDM shard attached site’s Docker network alias omopdb, Rock session resolves omopdb:5432 database. DataSHIELD resource (omop_demo.gibleed, format omop.dbi.db) tells dsOMOP open . resource definition therefore valid every site.
code: DataSHIELD client (host R)                   DSI · DSOpal · dsBaseClient · dsOMOPClient                                    │          ┌─────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┐          │ login(profile = "omop") │                          │    ┌─────▼─────┐              ┌─────▼─────┐              ┌─────▼─────┐    │  aphrc    │              │   dgh     │              │  iressef  │    │  Opal     │              │   Opal    │              │   Opal    │    │  + Rock   │              │  + Rock   │              │  + Rock   │   Rock "omop" profile    │  "omop"   │              │  "omop"   │              │  "omop"   │   = dsOMOP image    └─────┬─────┘              └─────┬─────┘              └─────┬─────┘          │ resource omop_demo.gibleed (format omop.dbi.db)     │    ┌─────▼─────┐              ┌─────▼─────┐              ┌─────▼─────┐    │ PostgreSQL│              │ PostgreSQL│              │ PostgreSQL│   alias "omopdb:5432"    │  cdm      │              │  cdm      │              │  cdm      │   on the site's    │ person%3=0│              │ person%3=1│              │ person%3=2│   docker network    └───────────┘              └───────────┘              └───────────┘

title: Federated Analysis using DataSHIELD
text: Apple Silicon / arm64: upstream Opal, Rock dsOMOP images published linux/amd64 , run emulation (slower, functional). dsOMOP image docker/ built amd64 reason.

title: Federated Analysis using DataSHIELD
text: Run five steps order. step reads machine-specific values previous one wrote sites.env (gitignored), nothing configure hand. Open site browser (URL printed step 2, also sites.env) log administrator / password inspect .
code: bash 1_setup/install_easy_opal.sh        # install easy-opal into ./.venv source .venv/bin/activate                #   (puts easy-opal on PATH) bash 2_opal_stacks/setup_sites.sh        # 3 × Opal + Rock("omop") + MongoDB bash 3_databases/setup_databases.sh      # 3 × PostgreSQL, GiBleed sharded by person bash 4_resources/setup_resources.sh      # the dsOMOP CDM resource on each site bash 5_client/setup_client.sh            # install client stack + run the analysis

title: Federated Analysis using DataSHIELD
text: Step 3 prints site’s shard size step 5 prints federated results. person shards partition cohort: Step 5 walks : schema exploration, federated row/person counts (per site pooled), -prevalent conditions, numeric value quantiles (drug-exposure days-supply), finally server-side person-level extraction analysed dsBaseClient (ds.dim, ds.colnames, ds.mean, ds.table).

title: Federated Analysis using DataSHIELD
text: Nothing patient-level ever leaves site. dsOMOP v2 performs statistical disclosure control every server response: standard DataSHIELD nfilter.* thresholds (minimum cell/subset counts, maximum factor levels, string-length limits), published package onto Rock omop profile, plus dsOMOP’s dsomop.* opt-outs; mandatory, non-disableable identifier strip applied every extracted table enters DataSHIELD session. dsOMOP v2 use datashield.privacyControlLevel (permissive/banana/avocado/... hierarchy dsBase mechanism rely ), privacy level needs set — see 4_resources/README.md.

title: Federated Analysis using DataSHIELD
text: Pinned reproducibility. Server-side packages come dsOMOP image (docker/); client-side packages installed step 5. dsOMOP image: davidsarrat/rock-dsomop-dswb-reproducibility:2.0.0 (linux/amd64).

title: Federated Analysis using DataSHIELD
text: GiBleed synthetic OMOP CDM 5.3 dataset — OHDSI/EunomiaDatasets @ 3efd533 (Apache-2.0). 2 694 synthetic persons; real patient data. OMOP CDM 5.3 DDL — OHDSI/CommonDataModel @ d83d48c (Apache-2.0). repository’s code released MIT License (see LICENSE).

title: Federated Analysis using DataSHIELD
text: Opal / MongoDB pinning. 2_opal_stacks/setup_sites.sh defaults OPAL_VERSION / MONGO_VERSION manifest versions (Opal 5.5.1, MongoDB 8.2.4); override either env var use different version. Plain HTTP used (OPAL_SSL=none) everything localhost, keeps client reproducible certificates. Set OPAL_SSL=self-signed HTTPS. Demo credentials intentional public: Opal administrator / password, PostgreSQL postgres / postgres. reuse anywhere real. recorded sites.env; dsOMOP resource never uses host port.

title: Federated Analysis using DataSHIELD
text: use package, please cite paper “Federated Analysis using DataSHIELD” (citation details added publication).
